text_content: P1063940 Tag A 32-bit identifier for a data element, represented as a pair of four-digit hexadecimal numbers, the "group" and the "element". If the "group" number is odd, the tag is for a private (manufacturer-specific) data element. Examples: (0010,0020) [Patient ID], (07FE,0010) [Pixel Data], (0019,0210) [private data element] Transfer Syntax The encoding used for exchange of DICOM information objects and messages. Examples: JPEG compressed (images), little endian explicit value representation. Unique Identifier (UID) A globally unique "dotted decimal" string that identifies a specific object or a class of objects; an ISO-8824 Object Identifier. Examples: Study Instance UID, SOP Class UID, SOP Instance UID. Value Representation (VR) The format type of an individual DICOM data element, such as text, an integer, a person's name, or a code.
